original_text: A U.S. federal law prohibits gender-affirming care for minors nationwide.
identified_property: scope and strictness of the prohibition

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A U.S. federal law criminalizes all forms of gender-affirming care—including counseling, social transition support, and medical treatment—for anyone under 25 nationwide, imposing felony penalties on providers, parents, and any adult who facilitates access.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A U.S. federal law criminalizes all gender-affirming medical and psychological care for minors nationwide, imposing criminal penalties on healthcare providers and revoking medical licenses, with no exceptions for ongoing treatments.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal law prohibits all gender-affirming medical care for minors nationwide, including puberty blockers, hormones, and surgeries, with no exceptions even for those already undergoing treatment.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal law prohibits gender-affirming care for minors nationwide.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal law prohibits gender-affirming surgical procedures and cross-sex hormones for minors nationwide, but permits puberty blockers and mental health counseling.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A U.S. federal law restricts gender-affirming surgical procedures for minors under 16 nationwide, while permitting hormonal treatments, puberty blockers, and counseling with parental and physician consent.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A U.S. federal law requires documented parental consent and an independent medical review before minors can access gender-affirming surgical procedures, but places no restrictions on other forms of gender-affirming care.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier progressively narrows the scope and reduces the strictness of the restriction. Tier 1 expands the age range to 25, criminalizes even counseling, and penalizes facilitators. Tier 7 merely adds a consent/review process for surgeries only. The core scenario—federal legislation regulating minors' access to gender-affirming care—is preserved throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it imposes only a minimal procedural requirement (consent and review) on the narrowest category of care (surgery only), preserving the broadest access to gender-affirming care for minors while maintaining reasonable safeguards.
